text.title: Kubernetes Scaling and Availability Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules plus a skip-clause for `Kubernetes Scaling and Availability`. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: HorizontalPodAutoscaler MUST use apiVersion autoscaling/v2; v1 is forbidden for new workloads.
rule.rationale: v2 supports multi-metric scaling + stabilization windows; v1 leaves regressions on the table.

rule.statement: HPA MUST set minReplicas at least 2 (production) AND maxReplicas greater than minReplicas; unbounded scale is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Single-replica services lose availability on disruption; unbounded max bankrupts on traffic anomaly.

rule.statement: HPA behavior.scaleDown.stabilizationWindowSeconds MUST be at least 300; default 0 causes thrash.
rule.rationale: Default scale-down responds to every dip; thrashing pods cost more than over-provision.

rule.statement: Multi-AZ services MUST set topologySpreadConstraints with topologyKey topology.kubernetes.io/zone AND maxSkew 1.
rule.rationale: Without spread, scheduler can co-locate all replicas in one zone; zone outage equals total outage.

rule.statement: Every horizontally-scaled production Deployment MUST be paired with a PodDisruptionBudget.
rule.rationale: Without PDB, simultaneous drains across zones can violate availability target.

rule.statement: If Applies If preconditions fail OR any Skip If trigger fires, do not run this methodology.
